mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals deteriorating consumer spending power and corporate earnings pressure, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based demand contraction that forces equity valuations lower over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 20-day returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 50% over a 24-month sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and reduced corporate revenue growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter during economic expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during expansion phases yield positive 60-day forward returns in over 60% of observations, or the predictive IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5 percent, liquidity injections and yield curve steepening trigger rapid equity risk-on flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates wage stagnation and eroding consumer margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-RSAFS divergence signal generates positive 60-day returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in housing starts exceeding 10 percent during a credit-constrained environment signals a housing market correction that drags down financial sector earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines above 10 percent fail to predict negative 20-day returns with directional accuracy above 52% during credit-tight regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 280, inflation expectations force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL stays above 280, or the signal Rank IC drops below 0.02.
